config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru perlindungan terhadap hak dasar itulah yang mencegah penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan keadilan bagi tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan mengorbankan individu secara otomatis.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea is dangerous because it can become a blank check for government or a majority to trample free speech, due process, privacy, and minority rights in the name of the common good. Society does have legitimate interests in public safety and public health, but those should justify only narrow, accountable limits, not automatic priority. A healthy society protects the individual first and then demands that people cooperate within those protections.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal hak warga justru perlu dilindungi supaya negara tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama untuk keamanan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh prinsip proporsionalitas dan akuntabilitas, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan orang per orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap mendorong tanggung jawab sosial.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as written, because in the United States “society first” too easily turns into majority rule without meaningful limits. I am fine with society having priority in narrow cases like emergency public health measures, criminal law, or immediate safety threats, but those limits have to be specific, temporary, and reviewable. Once you start treating individual rights as secondary by default, you invite abuse against unpopular people, dissenters, and minorities. In my view, the burden should always be on society to justify any restriction on rights, not on individuals to prove they deserve them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang tidak perlu atau tidak adil. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan umum, tetapi pembatasannya harus jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dijadikan prinsip bahwa individu selalu nomor dua. Justru kalau hak dasar mudah dikalahkan atas nama kepentingan mayoritas, yang rentan akan paling cepat dirugikan. Menurut saya, kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi tidak boleh berdiri di atas pengorbanan hak asasi sebagai aturan umum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general principle, because in the United States it has too often been used to justify overreach by governments and majorities. The strongest point in the recent turns is that limits can be legitimate for safety, health, and order, but those limits must be narrow, temporary, and accountable rather than treating the individual as automatically second-class. When society’s interests are made the default priority, minorities, dissenters, and unpopular people are usually the first ones harmed. To me, the better rule is that rights come first, and society has to earn any exception with a clear and serious justification.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
